prompt:
We're retiring the Perl script that mirrors incidents from our internal ops tracker into ServiceNow, and I want a small dependable Python package our scheduled jobs can import. Stdlib only — no requests, no SDK pip installs.

Before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to confirm the CURRENT official ServiceNow documentation for the REST Table API (developer.servicenow.com / servicenow.com docs) — confirm the endpoint shapes under /api/now/table, the sysparm_* query parameters, the {"result": ...} envelope, the pagination headers, and the error body format. We keep our understanding of the contract pinned in docs/contract.json with provenance in docs/official_sources.json; those are protected fixtures. Compare what the live docs say against the fixture — if they disagree, say so explicitly in your summary and leave the fixtures and tests alone. In your final message cite the exact official URLs you actually read.

What I need, package snow_sync (at least client.py, errors.py, sync.py plus __init__.py — split further if you want):

- snow_sync.client.TableClient(instance_url, username, password, sleep=time.sleep, max_retries=3)
  - get_records(table, query=None, fields=None, page_size=100) — follow offset pagination with sysparm_limit/sysparm_offset until the whole result set is in, return a list of dicts. Encode sysparm_query properly (carets and all) — the fake instance the tests spin up looks at the raw request line, sloppy encoding fails.
  - create_record(table, data) and update_record(table, sys_id, data) — JSON both ways, unwrap the result envelope, POST for new records and PATCH for updates against the record's sys_id.
  - Basic auth and Accept: application/json on every request. When ServiceNow answers with its error envelope, raise snow_sync.errors.SnowApiError carrying status_code/message/detail. On 429 honor Retry-After by calling the injected sleep, retry the same request up to max_retries, then raise snow_sync.errors.RateLimitError (subclass of SnowApiError, with .retry_after). Credentials must never show up in exception text, and never forward our Authorization header to a different origin if a response redirects elsewhere.

- snow_sync.sync.IncidentSyncer(client, key_field="correlation_id") with .sync(desired) returning a report with .created/.updated/.skipped counts and .sys_ids mapping correlation ids to ServiceNow sys_ids. One batched lookup query per run (the exact query and field projection we agreed with the ServiceNow team is in docs/contract.json), POST records that don't exist, PATCH only the fields that actually differ on ones that do, keep sys_id identity stable, and a rerun with unchanged input must be a pure no-op — skips only, zero write requests.

test_snow_sync.py is the acceptance contract; it runs a loopback fake instance, so no real instance and no real credentials are ever involved. Run python3 test_snow_sync.py until green. Don't touch the test file or anything under docs/.

research.interface: ServiceNow REST Table API, /api/now/table (unversioned = latest; Australia release documentation)

official_sources.title: Table API - ServiceNow API reference (Australia release)
official_sources.used_for: current-release confirmation of GET/POST/PATCH /api/now/table/{tableName}, the sysparm_query/sysparm_fields/sysparm_limit/sysparm_offset parameters, the {"result": ...} response envelope, and the Link and X-Total-Count pagination headers

official_sources.title: Table API - ServiceNow API reference (Zurich release)
official_sources.used_for: full method and parameter tables: sysparm_limit default 10000, sysparm_offset default 0, display-value/reference-link/no-count/suppress-pagination parameters, Accept/Content-Type requirements, status codes 200/201/204/400/404, Location header on POST

research.language_rationale: ServiceNow publishes no official Python SDK for the Table API; the documented integration surface is plain REST over /api/now/table, and Python's stdlib urllib is the idiomatic zero-dependency way to call it from scheduled sync jobs.
